news.title: Gurugram civic body plans new construction waste plant by February 2027
news.para: Gurugram is set to significantly boost its construction and demolition waste management with a new 400-tonne-per-day processing plant slated for 2027. The city is expanding its collection network, with new centres opening soon.  MCG aims to sell a substantial amount of processed waste to both government and private sectors, while also planning public awareness campaigns to encourage its use in municipal projects.

news.title: Ahmedabad civic body to waive property tax on parking spaces if malls, hotels open them to public
news.para: Ahmedabad Municipal Corporation is offering a 100% property tax exemption on parking areas to commercial establishments in exchange for opening their private basements to the public. This initiative aims to clear congested streets, following high court directives. Businesses have 60 days to comply, with potential new taxes on parking areas if they refuse. Concerns regarding security and customer displacement are being addressed with flexible proposals.

news.title: Telangana government cracks down on illegal construction & demolition dumping
news.para: Hyderabad's government is cracking down on illegal construction waste dumping. New rules now mandate developers to manage demolition debris responsibly, requiring undertakings against illegal dumping and proof of recycling partnerships. Large construction projects and road works must now incorporate recycled C&D waste, promoting sustainable practices and cleaner urban spaces across key municipal areas.

news.title: Maharashtra: Vertical property cards proposal awaits legal clearance, likely to be discussed in assembly session
news.para: Maharashtra is poised to introduce vertical property cards for apartment owners, a move expected to be discussed in the upcoming state legislature session. This groundbreaking initiative aims to provide individual ownership records for flats, enhancing transparency and simplifying transactions.  Currently awaiting final legal clearance, the proposal, if approved, will create a separate revenue record for each unit, marking a significant modernization of property records.

news.title: MahaRERA must use tech, AI to track, audit project compliance: Lawyers
news.para: MahaRERA's penalties on developers for delayed reports raise concerns about its oversight. Experts argue the regulator lacks mechanisms to verify developer disclosures, suggesting random inspections and AI monitoring. While MahaRERA prioritizes grievance redressal, critics point to potential loopholes in fund protection and the need for independent verification of project progress and financial statements to safeguard homebuyers.

news.title: Tech Mahindra leases four lakh sq ft office space in Hyderabad
news.para: In a major real estate development, Tech Mahindra has secured 4 lakh sq ft of office space in Hyderabad with a 10-year lease, reinforcing the city's position as a tech hub. This decision reflects the vibrant demand for premium office spaces among technology firms. The company's investment highlights Hyderabad's attractiveness as a primary location for large corporations looking for seamless expansion and reliable long-term occupancy.

news.title: Japan's NIFCO leases 5.13-acre plot in Maharashtra for manufacturing plant
news.para: NIFCO India is expanding its manufacturing capabilities with a new 5.13-acre plant in Shendra MIDC, Chhatrapati Sambhaji Nagar. This marks the automotive component maker's fourth facility in India, aiming to meet rising domestic demand from car manufacturers. The move underscores India's strategic importance for NIFCO and highlights the growing interest from Japanese firms in the country's industrial sector.

news.title: Dalmia Bharat plans to raise ₹4,000 crore; targets 110-130 MTPA cement capacity by FY31
news.para: Dalmia Bharat is set to raise up to Rs 4,000 crore to fuel its ambitious expansion, aiming to boost manufacturing capacity to 110-130 million tonnes per annum by FY31. This strategic move, involving acquisitions and organic growth, aligns with projected industry demand driven by infrastructure and housing. The company is also focusing on premium products and expanding its pan-India presence, reinforcing its commitment to national development.

news.title: Raipur: 84,000 tax defaulters face action as RMC chases ₹400-crore revenue target
news.para: Raipur Municipal Corporation is intensifying efforts to recover over ₹400 crore in property tax, with a June 30 deadline for a 6.25% rebate.  Around 84,000 defaulters face stringent action as the civic body leverages GIS technology to identify evaders. This drive aims to bolster financial autonomy and ensure sustained civic services, with officials emphasizing the importance of municipal self-reliance.

news.title: Over 100 builders eye housing projects in Prayagraj; 2,000 flats planned
news.para: Prayagraj's real estate is set for a boom with over 100 builders nationwide showing interest in developing group housing projects. This initiative by the Prayagraj Development Authority promises to significantly expand housing options, with an estimated 2,000 flats to be built.  The strong builder response signals growing demand, ensuring competitive prices and more choices for residents in the 'Kumbh city'.

news.title: Maharashtra government cuts land survey fees, sets strict timelines
news.para: Maharashtra has significantly slashed land survey charges, especially for family partitions, and introduced strict deadlines to streamline processes. Regular surveys will now take 90 days, with expedited ones in 30.  These reforms aim to curb delays, enhance transparency, and reduce corruption, offering much-needed relief to citizens amidst rising property transactions and disputes across the state.

news.title: Five private-run property registration hubs to open in August, one in Pune
news.para: Maharashtra's registration department is launching five private model sub-registrar offices in August, including one in Pune, as part of a plan for 60 such centers via public-private partnership. These modern facilities, modeled on Passport Seva Kendras, will offer improved infrastructure and services for an additional fee. While private operators manage facilities, government officials retain all statutory powers, ensuring continued regulatory control.

news.title: Bhopal: EOW books Hilltop Society office-bearers in multi-crore plot fraud case
news.para: Bhopal's Economic Offences Wing has filed a fraud case against office-bearers of the Hilltop Housing Cooperative Society. For over four decades, the society allegedly collected crores from hundreds of members for promised plots but failed to deliver possession. Investigations revealed evidence of cheating, forgery, and criminal conspiracy, with members facing years of delays and alleged manipulation of records, even after land acquisition by the MP Housing Board.

news.title: Institutional ownership narrows office supply as outright demand holds up
news.para: India’s office real estate market is shifting towards a leasing-led model, with developers, REITs and institutional investors increasingly retaining assets instead of selling them, reducing the availability of premium office spaces for outright purchase.

news.title: Chennai development body to exit construction, stick to urban planning
news.para: Chennai Metropolitan Development Authority (CMDA) is exiting construction after a ₹3,000 crore expenditure led to a ₹1,500 crore deficit.  The authority, which has exhausted its corpus, will refocus on its core mandate of urban planning, approvals, and creating new neighborhoods.  This strategic shift aims to revive CMDA's financial health and return to its foundational role in city development.

news.title: As flights take off at Noida airport, focus now shifts to Aerocity
news.para: Noida International Airport's launch is igniting a massive transformation, turning surrounding land into a vibrant aerotropolis.  Plans include hotels, malls, and business districts, creating a self-contained airport city.  This development, alongside significant industrial growth, is poised to generate thousands of jobs and establish the region as a major investment hub for the National Capital Region.

news.title: Over 41 lakh houses built for poor: Bihar IPRD minister
news.para: Bihar has achieved a significant milestone, constructing over 41.56 lakh permanent homes for the underprivileged since the 2016-17 fiscal year. This initiative, a revamped version of the Indira Awas Yojana now known as Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana (Gramin), provides crucial financial aid of Rs 1.20 lakh per beneficiary. The program aims to transform the lives of countless homeless families by enabling them to own a safe and dignified dwelling.

news.title: Over 300 beneficiaries in Jamshedpur receive keys of new homes developed under PMAY
news.para: Over 300 families in Jamshedpur's Birsanagar received keys to their new homes under the Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana. Jharkhand's Urban Development Minister inaugurated the event online, highlighting the project's role in providing safe and dignified living for the economically weaker sections. This initiative marks a significant step in urban development, aiming to improve living standards and fulfill the government's 'home for all' commitment.

news.title: Odisha government issues SOP for apartment registration
news.para: Apartment registrations in Odisha are set for a major overhaul with a new Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) introduced by the revenue and housing departments. This move aims to eliminate confusion arising from existing laws, ensuring a clearer and standardized process for buyers. The SOP categorizes registrations, outlines required documents, and clarifies verification steps for different types of apartment sales, bringing much-needed transparency to property transactions.

news.title: India needs affordable housing push, rental reforms to reach $5.8 trillion realty market by 2047: Report
news.para: A KPMG-NAREDCO report has identified affordable housing, rental housing, RERA-IBC alignment and stronger RERA implementation as key policy priorities for India’s real estate sector. The report was released at NAREDCO Real Estate Conclave 2026.
